revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:藤原氏, en:Fujiwara clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/97299244 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 雑 == * 奥州藤原氏の話はどうしましょうねぇ。 --[[利用者:Kozawa|Kozawa]] 09:01 2003年12月20日 (UTC) * 不比等が長屋王を殺したように記述してあるが、長屋王が殺されたのは不比等の死後ではないですか？--[[利用者:61.23.86.19|61.23.86.19]] 2006年2月7日 (火) 05:12 (UTC) * 以下の文章は学術的でないので削除しました。特に個人的意見は「事実かも知れない記述」にも当たりません。もしご意見があればどうぞ。--[[利用者:Law soma|ろう]] 2006年4月26日 (水) 06:10 (UTC) 一部の説では、藤原氏はユダヤ系の家系であるといわれている。歴史上の藤原氏をみても、知力で国を動かした人物が多く見られる。個人的見解ではあるが、藤原氏の人物の中で、名のイニシャルがＭ、Ｙ、Ｔ、Ｉの様に左右対称の者がいる。たとえば、道長などである。このように左右対象の名が与えられたものがユダヤ人である可能性が高い。当時は幼名が与えられ、その後名が与えられた。これは、ユダヤ人がミドルネームを与えられる文化に酷似している。 * 現代の藤原氏、のところですが、出典不明瞭なので要出典を貼りました。見づらくて申し訳ありませんが、分限帳なり論文なり提示してもらいたいです。歴史の本やウェブサイトでは藤原姓は典型的明治新姓のように書かれているので、意地悪ではなく大変興味があります。--[[利用者:125.172.115.34|125.172.115.34]] 2007年11月2日 (金) 12:15 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 女系 == 貴族社会は、男性が妻の家に通う「通い婚」が普通でした。なので、系譜に母親が判る表記を追加してはどうでしょうか？藤原北家の権勢が娘の婚姻と出産・育児を媒介にしたものでもありますし。--[[特別:投稿記録/202.233.232.162|202.233.232.162]] 2008年12月11日 (木) 19:26 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 藤原氏の家紋について == 藤原氏の代表家紋として、どれを掲載するのが最良でしょうか？ 私の過去の編集では、「代表」ということで「嫡流の近衛牡丹」と独断でしておりました（画像はなし）。今回、平成24年9月6日の版にて「下がり藤」と変更がなされました（何かコメントが欲しかったですが）ので、この際にと思い質問させて頂きました。確かに下がり藤には藤原氏のイメージが強いですが、[http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/hanasi/kamon_d.html こちら]（外部サイト）の青字を参考にしますと、「藤紋」自体が藤原氏にはむしろ少ない紋なのかと思われます。 末広がりすぎるので何も載せない、というのが1つの解のような気もしますが。こちらを読まれた方から何かコメントを下されば幸いです。--[[利用者:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]（[[利用者‐会話:Saigen Jiro|会話]]） 2012年9月6日 (木) 12:11 (UTC) 武家藤原氏の末裔でございます。基本的にはあくまでも北家嫡流の摂関家筆頭の近衛家が家紋として「近衛牡丹」を使用しております。その他の摂関家も牡丹や藤を使用しております。なのでこの記事（鎌足-藤原四兄弟）の藤原氏の代表家紋としては下がり藤を使用しているので「近衛牡丹」は使いません。 また、藤原は本性・氏（明治以前/氏は各々で異なる）であり、家名や苗字ではありません。なので我々のような藤原氏の後裔でも氏や家名・苗字が異なりますので、家紋も当然変わります。よって必然的に全国に散らばる公家・武家藤原氏の後裔一族には「藤紋」が少なくなります。 この記事に関して言えば、下がり藤を代紋としておくことが最良と思われます。 {{Unsigned-IPuser|114.148.48.191|2012年9月14日 (金) 8:53(UTC)|[[利用者:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]}} : 114.148.48.191さん、ご意見ありがとうございます。私の意見なのですが、鎌足-藤原四兄弟の時代には、まだ家紋が生まれていないのではないですよね？　そして家紋が生まれた時代以降は、四兄弟から派生した堂上各家はリンク先の通り「下がり藤」を使用しておりません。各家からさらに武家が派生し、彼らが藤原氏にルーツを求めた結果ようやく「下がり藤」が使われたに過ぎません。後世の枝葉末端の家が用いた「下がり藤」よりも、藤原氏主流の家紋を重視するべきではないでしょうか？　114.148.48.191さんの説明では、なぜ「下がり藤」が選択されるべきなのかが伝わってこないのですが…。 : とりあえず私の意見を申し上げました。ですが、春日大社が神紋に下がり藤を用いているということは、やっぱり下がり藤が藤原氏を代表するのですかね。春日大社に同意ということで、質問のテンプレを外させて頂きます。お騒がせいたしました。--[[利用者:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]（[[利用者‐会話:Saigen Jiro|会話]]） 2012年9月18日 (火) 01:23 (UTC) :誰と関係があるんでしょうか？--[[特別:投稿記録/2404:7A81:4140:A200:9C80:D932:9D02:58B|2404:7A81:4140:A200:9C80:D932:9D02:58B]] 2023年6月2日 (金) 22:51 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1268094544 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|vital=yes|listas=Fujiwara clan|blp=no|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|history=y|nobility=y}} {{WikiProject Genealogy|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Biography|royalty-work-group=yes}} }} ==Moved from [[Wikipedia:Redirects for deletion]]==
:''The following discussion is closed. {{red|'''Please do not modify it.'''}} Subsequent comments should be made in a new section.'' * [[Fujiwara clan]] -> [[Fujiwara family]]; to move the latter to the former (''Fujiwara clan'' has two revisions). It is inaccurate to call it family. After moving, I will put [[Fujiwara family]] again as a redirect.--[[User:Aphaia|Aphaea]] 12:20, 29 Oct 2004 (UTC) ** This is probably the right move; I'll check some of my reference books. [[User:Jnc|Noel]] 16:50, 29 Oct 2004 (UTC) ** Done. -- User:Docu ** This appears to have been a mistake. A Google search shows "Fujiwara family" is more common (2,080) than "Fujiwara clan" (915). In addition, the Britannica has them under [http://www.britannica.com/ebc/article?tocId=9365038 "Fujiwara family"]. [[User:Jnc|Noel]] [[User_talk:Jnc|(talk)]] 16:35, 7 Nov 2004 (UTC) ***If so, the article should be updated, then moved back. -- User:Docu ****[[Fujiwara family]] has no history, so this can be moved without needing to delete any redirects. Therefore, I've removed it from [[Wikipedia:Redirects for deletion|RfD]]. [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested moves]] would be a better place to discuss this that RfD. [[User:Angela|Angela]][[user talk:Angela|.]] :''The above discussion is preserved as an archive. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section.''
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==CfD== Removed cfdnotice, cfd has completed. --[[User:Kbdank71|Kbdank71]] 17:03, 9 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Comments== Sorry; I hadn't noticed this old discussion. Somewhere (I wish I could find it) on either the talk page of [[WP:J|WikiProject Japan]] or [[WP:MOS-JA]] we've discussed the logic of setting a standard for "clan" over "family". Standardization is a good thing. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 00:37, 22 April 2007 (UTC) :Google searches should be used with caution, and I wouldn't trust anything the Brittanica Encyclopedia has to say about Japan. It is notoriously outdated and badly written. Having said that, the question of clan over family should be answered with respect to the clan/family in question, and not based on some arbitrarily imposed standard. Assuming family refers to a limited set of blood/marriage ties and clan refers to ties by a combination of blood and mere association (as well as geographic location, in some cases), I think there are good arguments for referring to Fujiwara as a family, to be distinguished from true clans like the Sogas and the Mononobe. Although the most famous of the Sogas form a direct line of descent from [[Soga no Umako|Uamko]] to [[Soga no Emishi|Emishi]] to [[Soga no Iruka|Iruka]], my understanding is that they hail from a larger clan who don't necessarily all share blood ties. The Fujiwaras, on the other hand, trace their ancestry back to the first Fujiwara, [[Nakatomi no Kamatari]]. On the other hand, family also seems to imply a limited group, where the Fujiwaras comprise people spanning generations scattered throughout Japan. Therefore, it seems a bit of a stretch to consider them a "family". And I think this goes for the Minamotos and the Taira as well. On the balance, I would opt to keep these as "clans", rather than "families".-[[User:Jefu|Jefu]] 05:07, 23 April 2007 (UTC) ::Well, the Minamoto and Taira began as honorary names granted by Emperors to court nobles (''kuge''), many of whom I believe were related to the Imperial family in one way or another, and not necessarily particularly closely related to one another. Those two in particular I would definitely call clans, as they had a great many branch families (Seiwa Genji, Kawachi Genji, Kammu Heishi... the Ashikaga, Nitta, Takeda and Tokugawa claimed descent from the Minamoto, the Chiba, Miura, Hatakeyama, and Hojo from the Taira). ... It's one of those difficult things with some subtleties to the connotations and meanings of the terms, "clan" vs "family". [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 08:34, 23 April 2007 (UTC) :::I am late and I'm no specialist on this matter, either. But I added a bit in the lead to clarify how Fujiwara clan differs from other kuge family like [[Konoe family]], and why the article ought to be named Fujiwara clan not Fujiwara family. The distinction may be technical but seemingly significant, as this sort of technicality is all noble people care. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] 11:31, 16 September 2007 (UTC) The use of the word clan is also inaccurate and many historians advise against using it. The Fujiwara were an extended family in the sense that its members were related by blood, marriage, or adoption, so that I don't think the word "family" is entirely misleading. It's not perfect, but not wrong. Perhaps "lineage group" is better. At the very least, the article should indicate that there is no ideal term for uji and that the word clan is controversial. Michelle Li :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Regents table == {| border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2" |- ! Regent !! Lived !! Sessho !! Kampaku |- | [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Yoshifusa]] || [[804]]–[[872]] || [[866]]–[[872]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Mototsune|Mototsune]] || [[836]]–[[891]] || [[876]]–[[884]] || ([[887]]–[[890]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadahira|Tadahira]] || [[880]]–[[949]] || [[930]]–[[941]] || [[941]]–[[949]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Saneyori|Saneyori]] || [[900]]–[[970]] || [[969]]–[[970]] || [[967]]–[[969]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Koretada|Koretada]] || [[924]]–[[972]] || [[970]]–[[972]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Kanemichi|Kanemichi]] || [[925]]–[[977]] || — || [[973]]–[[977]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Yoritada|Yoritada]] || [[924]]–[[989]] || — || [[977]]–[[986]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Kaneie|Kaneie]] || [[929]]–[[990]] || [[986]]–[[990]] || [[990]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michitaka|Michitaka]] || [[953]]–[[995]] || [[990]]–[[993]] || [[993]]–[[995]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michikane|Michikane]] || [[961]]–[[995]] || — || [[995]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michinaga|Michinaga]] || [[966]]–[[1028]] || [[1016]]–[[1017]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Yorimichi]] || [[990]]–[[1074]] || [[1017]]–[[1020]] || [[1020]]–[[1068]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Norimichi|Norimichi]] || [[997]]–[[1075]] || — || [[1068]]–[[1075]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Morozane|Morozane]] || [[1042]]–[[1101]] || [[1087]]–[[1091]] || [[1075]]–[[1087]], [[1091]]–[[1094]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Moromichi|Moromichi]] || [[1062]]–[[1099]] || — || [[1094]]–[[1099]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadazane|Tadazane]] || [[1078]]–[[1162]] || [[1107]]–[[1114]] || [[1106]]–[[1107]], [[1114]]–[[1121]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadamichi|Tadamichi]] || [[1097]]–[[1164]] || [[1123]]–[[1129]], [[1142]]–[[1151]] || [[1121]]–[[1123]], [[1129]]–[[1142]], [[1151]]–[[1158]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Motozane|Motozane]] || [[1143]]–[[1166]] || [[1165]]–[[1166]] || [[1158]]–[[1165]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Motofusa|Motofusa]] || [[1144]]–[[1230]] || [[1166]]–[[1173]] || [[1173]]–[[1179]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Moroie|Moroie]] || [[1172]]–[[1238]] || [[1184]] || — |} The above was moved from the main page. Since it is duplicate to the list found in [[Sesshō and Kampaku]], I'm not sure about the point of the table. Maybe some different kind of a list can be useful, but what could that be? -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] 14:25, 20 September 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Branch families == Though not explained in the article (perhaps it should be), it seems obvious to me that the Hokke, Nanke, and Kyōke are "northern house", "southern house", and "house of the capital", respectively. I know the Hokke were based in the north, in [[Hiraizumi]], and can assume that the Kyōke (which I never heard of before today) was based in Kyoto (Heian-kyō), but what's the story with the Nanke? What is the kanji for Shikike, and what can we add about that branch? Thank you. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] ([[User talk:LordAmeth|talk]]) 17:55, 11 March 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/97299244 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 雑 == * 奥州藤原氏の話はどうしましょうねぇ。 --[[User:Kozawa|Kozawa]] 09:01 2003年12月20日 (UTC) * 不比等が長屋王を殺したように記述してあるが、長屋王が殺されたのは不比等の死後ではないですか？--[[User:61.23.86.19|61.23.86.19]] 2006年2月7日 (火) 05:12 (UTC) * 以下の文章は学術的でないので削除しました。特に個人的意見は「事実かも知れない記述」にも当たりません。もしご意見があればどうぞ。--[[User:Law soma|ろう]] 2006年4月26日 (水) 06:10 (UTC) 一部の説では、藤原氏はユダヤ系の家系であるといわれている。歴史上の藤原氏をみても、知力で国を動かした人物が多く見られる。個人的見解ではあるが、藤原氏の人物の中で、名のイニシャルがＭ、Ｙ、Ｔ、Ｉの様に左右対称の者がいる。たとえば、道長などである。このように左右対象の名が与えられたものがユダヤ人である可能性が高い。当時は幼名が与えられ、その後名が与えられた。これは、ユダヤ人がミドルネームを与えられる文化に酷似している。 * 現代の藤原氏、のところですが、出典不明瞭なので要出典を貼りました。見づらくて申し訳ありませんが、分限帳なり論文なり提示してもらいたいです。歴史の本やウェブサイトでは藤原姓は典型的明治新姓のように書かれているので、意地悪ではなく大変興味があります。--[[User:125.172.115.34|125.172.115.34]] 2007年11月2日 (金) 12:15 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 女系 == 貴族社会は、男性が妻の家に通う「通い婚」が普通でした。なので、系譜に母親が判る表記を追加してはどうでしょうか？藤原北家の権勢が娘の婚姻と出産・育児を媒介にしたものでもありますし。--[[特別:投稿記録/202.233.232.162|202.233.232.162]] 2008年12月11日 (木) 19:26 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 藤原氏の家紋について == 藤原氏の代表家紋として、どれを掲載するのが最良でしょうか？ 私の過去の編集では、「代表」ということで「嫡流の近衛牡丹」と独断でしておりました（画像はなし）。今回、平成24年9月6日の版にて「下がり藤」と変更がなされました（何かコメントが欲しかったですが）ので、この際にと思い質問させて頂きました。確かに下がり藤には藤原氏のイメージが強いですが、[http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/hanasi/kamon_d.html こちら]（外部サイト）の青字を参考にしますと、「藤紋」自体が藤原氏にはむしろ少ない紋なのかと思われます。 末広がりすぎるので何も載せない、というのが1つの解のような気もしますが。こちらを読まれた方から何かコメントを下されば幸いです。--[[User:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]（[[利用者‐会話:Saigen Jiro|会話]]） 2012年9月6日 (木) 12:11 (UTC) 武家藤原氏の末裔でございます。基本的にはあくまでも北家嫡流の摂関家筆頭の近衛家が家紋として「近衛牡丹」を使用しております。その他の摂関家も牡丹や藤を使用しております。なのでこの記事（鎌足-藤原四兄弟）の藤原氏の代表家紋としては下がり藤を使用しているので「近衛牡丹」は使いません。 また、藤原は本性・氏（明治以前/氏は各々で異なる）であり、家名や苗字ではありません。なので我々のような藤原氏の後裔でも氏や家名・苗字が異なりますので、家紋も当然変わります。よって必然的に全国に散らばる公家・武家藤原氏の後裔一族には「藤紋」が少なくなります。 この記事に関して言えば、下がり藤を代紋としておくことが最良と思われます。 {{Unsigned-IPuser|114.148.48.191|2012年9月14日 (金) 8:53(UTC)|[[User:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]}} : 114.148.48.191さん、ご意見ありがとうございます。私の意見なのですが、鎌足-藤原四兄弟の時代には、まだ家紋が生まれていないのではないですよね？　そして家紋が生まれた時代以降は、四兄弟から派生した堂上各家はリンク先の通り「下がり藤」を使用しておりません。各家からさらに武家が派生し、彼らが藤原氏にルーツを求めた結果ようやく「下がり藤」が使われたに過ぎません。後世の枝葉末端の家が用いた「下がり藤」よりも、藤原氏主流の家紋を重視するべきではないでしょうか？　114.148.48.191さんの説明では、なぜ「下がり藤」が選択されるべきなのかが伝わってこないのですが…。 : とりあえず私の意見を申し上げました。ですが、春日大社が神紋に下がり藤を用いているということは、やっぱり下がり藤が藤原氏を代表するのですかね。春日大社に同意ということで、質問のテンプレを外させて頂きます。お騒がせいたしました。--[[User:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]（[[利用者‐会話:Saigen Jiro|会話]]） 2012年9月18日 (火) 01:23 (UTC) :誰と関係があるんでしょうか？--[[特別:投稿記録/2404:7A81:4140:A200:9C80:D932:9D02:58B|2404:7A81:4140:A200:9C80:D932:9D02:58B]] 2023年6月2日 (金) 22:51 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1268094544 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|vital=yes|listas=Fujiwara clan|blp=no|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|history=y|nobility=y}} {{WikiProject Genealogy|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Biography|royalty-work-group=yes}} }} ==Moved from [[Wikipedia:Redirects for deletion]]==
:''The following discussion is closed. {{red|'''Please do not modify it.'''}} Subsequent comments should be made in a new section.'' * [[Fujiwara clan]] -> [[Fujiwara family]]; to move the latter to the former (''Fujiwara clan'' has two revisions). It is inaccurate to call it family. After moving, I will put [[Fujiwara family]] again as a redirect.--[[User:Aphaia|Aphaea]] 12:20, 29 Oct 2004 (UTC) ** This is probably the right move; I'll check some of my reference books. [[User:Jnc|Noel]] 16:50, 29 Oct 2004 (UTC) ** Done. -- User:Docu ** This appears to have been a mistake. A Google search shows "Fujiwara family" is more common (2,080) than "Fujiwara clan" (915). In addition, the Britannica has them under [http://www.britannica.com/ebc/article?tocId=9365038 "Fujiwara family"]. [[User:Jnc|Noel]] [[User_talk:Jnc|(talk)]] 16:35, 7 Nov 2004 (UTC) ***If so, the article should be updated, then moved back. -- User:Docu ****[[Fujiwara family]] has no history, so this can be moved without needing to delete any redirects. Therefore, I've removed it from [[Wikipedia:Redirects for deletion|RfD]]. [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested moves]] would be a better place to discuss this that RfD. [[User:Angela|Angela]][[user talk:Angela|.]] :''The above discussion is preserved as an archive. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section.''
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==CfD== Removed cfdnotice, cfd has completed. --[[User:Kbdank71|Kbdank71]] 17:03, 9 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Comments== Sorry; I hadn't noticed this old discussion. Somewhere (I wish I could find it) on either the talk page of [[WP:J|WikiProject Japan]] or [[WP:MOS-JA]] we've discussed the logic of setting a standard for "clan" over "family". Standardization is a good thing. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 00:37, 22 April 2007 (UTC) :Google searches should be used with caution, and I wouldn't trust anything the Brittanica Encyclopedia has to say about Japan. It is notoriously outdated and badly written. Having said that, the question of clan over family should be answered with respect to the clan/family in question, and not based on some arbitrarily imposed standard. Assuming family refers to a limited set of blood/marriage ties and clan refers to ties by a combination of blood and mere association (as well as geographic location, in some cases), I think there are good arguments for referring to Fujiwara as a family, to be distinguished from true clans like the Sogas and the Mononobe. Although the most famous of the Sogas form a direct line of descent from [[Soga no Umako|Uamko]] to [[Soga no Emishi|Emishi]] to [[Soga no Iruka|Iruka]], my understanding is that they hail from a larger clan who don't necessarily all share blood ties. The Fujiwaras, on the other hand, trace their ancestry back to the first Fujiwara, [[Nakatomi no Kamatari]]. On the other hand, family also seems to imply a limited group, where the Fujiwaras comprise people spanning generations scattered throughout Japan. Therefore, it seems a bit of a stretch to consider them a "family". And I think this goes for the Minamotos and the Taira as well. On the balance, I would opt to keep these as "clans", rather than "families".-[[User:Jefu|Jefu]] 05:07, 23 April 2007 (UTC) ::Well, the Minamoto and Taira began as honorary names granted by Emperors to court nobles (''kuge''), many of whom I believe were related to the Imperial family in one way or another, and not necessarily particularly closely related to one another. Those two in particular I would definitely call clans, as they had a great many branch families (Seiwa Genji, Kawachi Genji, Kammu Heishi... the Ashikaga, Nitta, Takeda and Tokugawa claimed descent from the Minamoto, the Chiba, Miura, Hatakeyama, and Hojo from the Taira). ... It's one of those difficult things with some subtleties to the connotations and meanings of the terms, "clan" vs "family". [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 08:34, 23 April 2007 (UTC) :::I am late and I'm no specialist on this matter, either. But I added a bit in the lead to clarify how Fujiwara clan differs from other kuge family like [[Konoe family]], and why the article ought to be named Fujiwara clan not Fujiwara family. The distinction may be technical but seemingly significant, as this sort of technicality is all noble people care. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] 11:31, 16 September 2007 (UTC) The use of the word clan is also inaccurate and many historians advise against using it. The Fujiwara were an extended family in the sense that its members were related by blood, marriage, or adoption, so that I don't think the word "family" is entirely misleading. It's not perfect, but not wrong. Perhaps "lineage group" is better. At the very least, the article should indicate that there is no ideal term for uji and that the word clan is controversial. Michelle Li :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Regents table == {| border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2" |- ! Regent !! Lived !! Sessho !! Kampaku |- | [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Yoshifusa]] || [[804]]–[[872]] || [[866]]–[[872]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Mototsune|Mototsune]] || [[836]]–[[891]] || [[876]]–[[884]] || ([[887]]–[[890]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadahira|Tadahira]] || [[880]]–[[949]] || [[930]]–[[941]] || [[941]]–[[949]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Saneyori|Saneyori]] || [[900]]–[[970]] || [[969]]–[[970]] || [[967]]–[[969]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Koretada|Koretada]] || [[924]]–[[972]] || [[970]]–[[972]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Kanemichi|Kanemichi]] || [[925]]–[[977]] || — || [[973]]–[[977]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Yoritada|Yoritada]] || [[924]]–[[989]] || — || [[977]]–[[986]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Kaneie|Kaneie]] || [[929]]–[[990]] || [[986]]–[[990]] || [[990]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michitaka|Michitaka]] || [[953]]–[[995]] || [[990]]–[[993]] || [[993]]–[[995]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michikane|Michikane]] || [[961]]–[[995]] || — || [[995]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michinaga|Michinaga]] || [[966]]–[[1028]] || [[1016]]–[[1017]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Yorimichi]] || [[990]]–[[1074]] || [[1017]]–[[1020]] || [[1020]]–[[1068]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Norimichi|Norimichi]] || [[997]]–[[1075]] || — || [[1068]]–[[1075]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Morozane|Morozane]] || [[1042]]–[[1101]] || [[1087]]–[[1091]] || [[1075]]–[[1087]], [[1091]]–[[1094]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Moromichi|Moromichi]] || [[1062]]–[[1099]] || — || [[1094]]–[[1099]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadazane|Tadazane]] || [[1078]]–[[1162]] || [[1107]]–[[1114]] || [[1106]]–[[1107]], [[1114]]–[[1121]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadamichi|Tadamichi]] || [[1097]]–[[1164]] || [[1123]]–[[1129]], [[1142]]–[[1151]] || [[1121]]–[[1123]], [[1129]]–[[1142]], [[1151]]–[[1158]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Motozane|Motozane]] || [[1143]]–[[1166]] || [[1165]]–[[1166]] || [[1158]]–[[1165]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Motofusa|Motofusa]] || [[1144]]–[[1230]] || [[1166]]–[[1173]] || [[1173]]–[[1179]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Moroie|Moroie]] || [[1172]]–[[1238]] || [[1184]] || — |} The above was moved from the main page. Since it is duplicate to the list found in [[Sesshō and Kampaku]], I'm not sure about the point of the table. Maybe some different kind of a list can be useful, but what could that be? -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] 14:25, 20 September 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Branch families == Though not explained in the article (perhaps it should be), it seems obvious to me that the Hokke, Nanke, and Kyōke are "northern house", "southern house", and "house of the capital", respectively. I know the Hokke were based in the north, in [[Hiraizumi]], and can assume that the Kyōke (which I never heard of before today) was based in Kyoto (Heian-kyō), but what's the story with the Nanke? What is the kanji for Shikike, and what can we add about that branch? Thank you. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] ([[User talk:LordAmeth|talk]]) 17:55, 11 March 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:wikipedia>Gryffindor]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Fujiwara_clan here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/97299244 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 雑 == * 奥州藤原氏の話はどうしましょうねぇ。 --[[User:Kozawa|Kozawa]] 09:01 2003年12月20日 (UTC) * 不比等が長屋王を殺したように記述してあるが、長屋王が殺されたのは不比等の死後ではないですか？--[[User:61.23.86.19|61.23.86.19]] 2006年2月7日 (火) 05:12 (UTC) * 以下の文章は学術的でないので削除しました。特に個人的意見は「事実かも知れない記述」にも当たりません。もしご意見があればどうぞ。--[[User:Law soma|ろう]] 2006年4月26日 (水) 06:10 (UTC) 一部の説では、藤原氏はユダヤ系の家系であるといわれている。歴史上の藤原氏をみても、知力で国を動かした人物が多く見られる。個人的見解ではあるが、藤原氏の人物の中で、名のイニシャルがＭ、Ｙ、Ｔ、Ｉの様に左右対称の者がいる。たとえば、道長などである。このように左右対象の名が与えられたものがユダヤ人である可能性が高い。当時は幼名が与えられ、その後名が与えられた。これは、ユダヤ人がミドルネームを与えられる文化に酷似している。 * 現代の藤原氏、のところですが、出典不明瞭なので要出典を貼りました。見づらくて申し訳ありませんが、分限帳なり論文なり提示してもらいたいです。歴史の本やウェブサイトでは藤原姓は典型的明治新姓のように書かれているので、意地悪ではなく大変興味があります。--[[User:125.172.115.34|125.172.115.34]] 2007年11月2日 (金) 12:15 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 女系 == 貴族社会は、男性が妻の家に通う「通い婚」が普通でした。なので、系譜に母親が判る表記を追加してはどうでしょうか？藤原北家の権勢が娘の婚姻と出産・育児を媒介にしたものでもありますし。--[[特別:投稿記録/202.233.232.162|202.233.232.162]] 2008年12月11日 (木) 19:26 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 藤原氏の家紋について == 藤原氏の代表家紋として、どれを掲載するのが最良でしょうか？ 私の過去の編集では、「代表」ということで「嫡流の近衛牡丹」と独断でしておりました（画像はなし）。今回、平成24年9月6日の版にて「下がり藤」と変更がなされました（何かコメントが欲しかったですが）ので、この際にと思い質問させて頂きました。確かに下がり藤には藤原氏のイメージが強いですが、[http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/hanasi/kamon_d.html こちら]（外部サイト）の青字を参考にしますと、「藤紋」自体が藤原氏にはむしろ少ない紋なのかと思われます。 末広がりすぎるので何も載せない、というのが1つの解のような気もしますが。こちらを読まれた方から何かコメントを下されば幸いです。--[[User:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]（[[利用者‐会話:Saigen Jiro|会話]]） 2012年9月6日 (木) 12:11 (UTC) 武家藤原氏の末裔でございます。基本的にはあくまでも北家嫡流の摂関家筆頭の近衛家が家紋として「近衛牡丹」を使用しております。その他の摂関家も牡丹や藤を使用しております。なのでこの記事（鎌足-藤原四兄弟）の藤原氏の代表家紋としては下がり藤を使用しているので「近衛牡丹」は使いません。 また、藤原は本性・氏（明治以前/氏は各々で異なる）であり、家名や苗字ではありません。なので我々のような藤原氏の後裔でも氏や家名・苗字が異なりますので、家紋も当然変わります。よって必然的に全国に散らばる公家・武家藤原氏の後裔一族には「藤紋」が少なくなります。 この記事に関して言えば、下がり藤を代紋としておくことが最良と思われます。 {{Unsigned-IPuser|114.148.48.191|2012年9月14日 (金) 8:53(UTC)|[[User:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]}} : 114.148.48.191さん、ご意見ありがとうございます。私の意見なのですが、鎌足-藤原四兄弟の時代には、まだ家紋が生まれていないのではないですよね？　そして家紋が生まれた時代以降は、四兄弟から派生した堂上各家はリンク先の通り「下がり藤」を使用しておりません。各家からさらに武家が派生し、彼らが藤原氏にルーツを求めた結果ようやく「下がり藤」が使われたに過ぎません。後世の枝葉末端の家が用いた「下がり藤」よりも、藤原氏主流の家紋を重視するべきではないでしょうか？　114.148.48.191さんの説明では、なぜ「下がり藤」が選択されるべきなのかが伝わってこないのですが…。 : とりあえず私の意見を申し上げました。ですが、春日大社が神紋に下がり藤を用いているということは、やっぱり下がり藤が藤原氏を代表するのですかね。春日大社に同意ということで、質問のテンプレを外させて頂きます。お騒がせいたしました。--[[User:Saigen Jiro|Saigen Jiro]]（[[利用者‐会話:Saigen Jiro|会話]]） 2012年9月18日 (火) 01:23 (UTC) :誰と関係があるんでしょうか？--[[特別:投稿記録/2404:7A81:4140:A200:9C80:D932:9D02:58B|2404:7A81:4140:A200:9C80:D932:9D02:58B]] 2023年6月2日 (金) 22:51 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1268094544 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|vital=yes|listas=Fujiwara clan|blp=no|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|history=y|nobility=y}} {{WikiProject Genealogy|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Biography|royalty-work-group=yes}} }} ==Moved from [[Wikipedia:Redirects for deletion]]==
:''The following discussion is closed. {{red|'''Please do not modify it.'''}} Subsequent comments should be made in a new section.'' * [[Fujiwara clan]] -> [[Fujiwara family]]; to move the latter to the former (''Fujiwara clan'' has two revisions). It is inaccurate to call it family. After moving, I will put [[Fujiwara family]] again as a redirect.--[[User:Aphaia|Aphaea]] 12:20, 29 Oct 2004 (UTC) ** This is probably the right move; I'll check some of my reference books. [[User:Jnc|Noel]] 16:50, 29 Oct 2004 (UTC) ** Done. -- User:Docu ** This appears to have been a mistake. A Google search shows "Fujiwara family" is more common (2,080) than "Fujiwara clan" (915). In addition, the Britannica has them under [http://www.britannica.com/ebc/article?tocId=9365038 "Fujiwara family"]. [[User:Jnc|Noel]] [[User_talk:Jnc|(talk)]] 16:35, 7 Nov 2004 (UTC) ***If so, the article should be updated, then moved back. -- User:Docu ****[[Fujiwara family]] has no history, so this can be moved without needing to delete any redirects. Therefore, I've removed it from [[Wikipedia:Redirects for deletion|RfD]]. [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested moves]] would be a better place to discuss this that RfD. [[User:Angela|Angela]][[user talk:Angela|.]] :''The above discussion is preserved as an archive. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section.''
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==CfD== Removed cfdnotice, cfd has completed. --[[User:Kbdank71|Kbdank71]] 17:03, 9 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Comments== Sorry; I hadn't noticed this old discussion. Somewhere (I wish I could find it) on either the talk page of [[WP:J|WikiProject Japan]] or [[WP:MOS-JA]] we've discussed the logic of setting a standard for "clan" over "family". Standardization is a good thing. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 00:37, 22 April 2007 (UTC) :Google searches should be used with caution, and I wouldn't trust anything the Brittanica Encyclopedia has to say about Japan. It is notoriously outdated and badly written. Having said that, the question of clan over family should be answered with respect to the clan/family in question, and not based on some arbitrarily imposed standard. Assuming family refers to a limited set of blood/marriage ties and clan refers to ties by a combination of blood and mere association (as well as geographic location, in some cases), I think there are good arguments for referring to Fujiwara as a family, to be distinguished from true clans like the Sogas and the Mononobe. Although the most famous of the Sogas form a direct line of descent from [[Soga no Umako|Uamko]] to [[Soga no Emishi|Emishi]] to [[Soga no Iruka|Iruka]], my understanding is that they hail from a larger clan who don't necessarily all share blood ties. The Fujiwaras, on the other hand, trace their ancestry back to the first Fujiwara, [[Nakatomi no Kamatari]]. On the other hand, family also seems to imply a limited group, where the Fujiwaras comprise people spanning generations scattered throughout Japan. Therefore, it seems a bit of a stretch to consider them a "family". And I think this goes for the Minamotos and the Taira as well. On the balance, I would opt to keep these as "clans", rather than "families".-[[User:Jefu|Jefu]] 05:07, 23 April 2007 (UTC) ::Well, the Minamoto and Taira began as honorary names granted by Emperors to court nobles (''kuge''), many of whom I believe were related to the Imperial family in one way or another, and not necessarily particularly closely related to one another. Those two in particular I would definitely call clans, as they had a great many branch families (Seiwa Genji, Kawachi Genji, Kammu Heishi... the Ashikaga, Nitta, Takeda and Tokugawa claimed descent from the Minamoto, the Chiba, Miura, Hatakeyama, and Hojo from the Taira). ... It's one of those difficult things with some subtleties to the connotations and meanings of the terms, "clan" vs "family". [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 08:34, 23 April 2007 (UTC) :::I am late and I'm no specialist on this matter, either. But I added a bit in the lead to clarify how Fujiwara clan differs from other kuge family like [[Konoe family]], and why the article ought to be named Fujiwara clan not Fujiwara family. The distinction may be technical but seemingly significant, as this sort of technicality is all noble people care. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] 11:31, 16 September 2007 (UTC) The use of the word clan is also inaccurate and many historians advise against using it. The Fujiwara were an extended family in the sense that its members were related by blood, marriage, or adoption, so that I don't think the word "family" is entirely misleading. It's not perfect, but not wrong. Perhaps "lineage group" is better. At the very least, the article should indicate that there is no ideal term for uji and that the word clan is controversial. Michelle Li :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Regents table == {| border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2" |- ! Regent !! Lived !! Sessho !! Kampaku |- | [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Yoshifusa]] || [[804]]–[[872]] || [[866]]–[[872]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Mototsune|Mototsune]] || [[836]]–[[891]] || [[876]]–[[884]] || ([[887]]–[[890]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadahira|Tadahira]] || [[880]]–[[949]] || [[930]]–[[941]] || [[941]]–[[949]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Saneyori|Saneyori]] || [[900]]–[[970]] || [[969]]–[[970]] || [[967]]–[[969]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Koretada|Koretada]] || [[924]]–[[972]] || [[970]]–[[972]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Kanemichi|Kanemichi]] || [[925]]–[[977]] || — || [[973]]–[[977]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Yoritada|Yoritada]] || [[924]]–[[989]] || — || [[977]]–[[986]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Kaneie|Kaneie]] || [[929]]–[[990]] || [[986]]–[[990]] || [[990]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michitaka|Michitaka]] || [[953]]–[[995]] || [[990]]–[[993]] || [[993]]–[[995]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michikane|Michikane]] || [[961]]–[[995]] || — || [[995]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Michinaga|Michinaga]] || [[966]]–[[1028]] || [[1016]]–[[1017]] || — |- | [[Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Yorimichi]] || [[990]]–[[1074]] || [[1017]]–[[1020]] || [[1020]]–[[1068]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Norimichi|Norimichi]] || [[997]]–[[1075]] || — || [[1068]]–[[1075]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Morozane|Morozane]] || [[1042]]–[[1101]] || [[1087]]–[[1091]] || [[1075]]–[[1087]], [[1091]]–[[1094]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Moromichi|Moromichi]] || [[1062]]–[[1099]] || — || [[1094]]–[[1099]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadazane|Tadazane]] || [[1078]]–[[1162]] || [[1107]]–[[1114]] || [[1106]]–[[1107]], [[1114]]–[[1121]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Tadamichi|Tadamichi]] || [[1097]]–[[1164]] || [[1123]]–[[1129]], [[1142]]–[[1151]] || [[1121]]–[[1123]], [[1129]]–[[1142]], [[1151]]–[[1158]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Motozane|Motozane]] || [[1143]]–[[1166]] || [[1165]]–[[1166]] || [[1158]]–[[1165]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Motofusa|Motofusa]] || [[1144]]–[[1230]] || [[1166]]–[[1173]] || [[1173]]–[[1179]] |- | [[Fujiwara no Moroie|Moroie]] || [[1172]]–[[1238]] || [[1184]] || — |} The above was moved from the main page. Since it is duplicate to the list found in [[Sesshō and Kampaku]], I'm not sure about the point of the table. Maybe some different kind of a list can be useful, but what could that be? -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] 14:25, 20 September 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Branch families == Though not explained in the article (perhaps it should be), it seems obvious to me that the Hokke, Nanke, and Kyōke are "northern house", "southern house", and "house of the capital", respectively. I know the Hokke were based in the north, in [[Hiraizumi]], and can assume that the Kyōke (which I never heard of before today) was based in Kyoto (Heian-kyō), but what's the story with the Nanke? What is the kanji for Shikike, and what can we add about that branch? Thank you. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] ([[User talk:LordAmeth|talk]]) 17:55, 11 March 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:27, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
